methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Ocean Infinity, a Growth-stage maritime technology company founded by Oliver Plunkett, has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a limited comparable universe: one identified private peer (Orbital Marine Power) and zero public comparables in the ocean and maritime sector, reflecting the nascent nature of frontier technology in this domain.

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier to the $100M total capital raised proxy, a standard framework for Growth-stage companies transitioning toward later funding rounds. This method yields an implied valuation of $120M. Given the sparse comparable data and single-method analytical foundation, the triangulation range extends from $102M (floor incorporating baseline capital) to $138M (ceiling reflecting modest upside from stage advancement assumptions).

The median valuation across this range is $120M. Key uncertainties center on the reliability of total raised as a valuation proxy without corroborating market comparable evidence, the applicability of standard stage multipliers to frontier maritime technology where precedent is limited, and the absence of public market peers to validate private valuations. A more robust analysis would benefit from additional private comparables and revenue/operational metrics from Ocean Infinity itself. The current range should be treated as indicative pending deeper operational due diligence.
